meta.title: Safety Audit — Permit-to-Work System, Maintenance Shop, Plant 3
meta.subtitle: Management-system audit vs ISO 45001 8.1 + the org PTW procedure — de-identified; competent-person review required
meta.author: HSE Department
meta.deid_notice: All personnel are referred to by role label. Identifiers in the audited evidence (names, signatures, training/injury records) were pseudonymised before any assessment; the re-identification key is held separately and is not part of this document. No injury/illness cell of fewer than 5 individuals is published. This audit is a point-in-time sample; a competent person must validate it.

sections.text: Management-system audit of the permit-to-work (PTW) system at the Plant 3 maintenance shop (issuance → isolation → sign-off → close-out; hot-work permits excluded), audited against ISO 45001 clause 8.1 (operational control) and the org's PTW procedure, audit method per ISO 45001 9.2. Of 6 assessed criteria, 3 conform, 1 is a major nonconformity, 1 a minor nonconformity, and 1 an opportunity for improvement. The highest-risk finding is the absence of an enforced isolation-verification step before sign-off (NC-Major, High). Every finding below traces to specific objective evidence; every nonconformity is risk-rated; every corrective action is hierarchy-of-controls-ranked with a named owner and date. This report is de-identified throughout.

items: Scope: the PTW system at the Plant 3 maintenance shop — issuance, isolation, sign-off, close-out. Boundary: hot-work permits excluded.
Criteria (Q-Crit): ISO 45001 clause 8.1 operational control + the org's PTW procedure (management-system audit type).
Audit method: ISO 45001 clause 9.2 (internal audit) — scope → criteria → evidence → findings → conformity rating → corrective action.
Methodology: a sample of 12 issued permits across 3 shifts; an isolation-register extract; two interview summaries by role; direct observation of one issuance.

sections.text: Key findings — clause-by-clause (each traced to evidence)

rows.title: 8.1 / PTW-1 — Permit issuance authorisation
rows.description: Conformity. The PTW procedure requires a named authorised issuer; the sampled permits all carried an issuer role + the work scope.

rows.title: 8.1 / PTW-2 — Isolation verification before sign-off
rows.description: Nonconformity — Major. The procedure does not mandate an independent isolation-verification step before the permit is signed off; 4 of 12 sampled permits were signed off with no recorded isolation check. A systemic gap, not an isolated lapse.
rows.evidence: E-1 (4/12 permits, no isolation record); E-3 (isolation-register extract — gaps); E-4 (PTW procedure §5, no verification step)

rows.title: 8.1 / PTW-3 — Permit close-out & sign-back
rows.description: Nonconformity — Minor. Close-out was performed but 2 of 12 permits lacked the final sign-back signature — an isolated lapse against an otherwise-followed step.
rows.evidence: E-1 (2/12 permits, no sign-back); E-5 (interview, Supervisor role)

rows.title: 8.1 / PTW-4 — Competence of issuers
rows.description: Conformity. Sampled issuers held current PTW-authoriser training; records were in date.
rows.evidence: E-6 (training matrix, de-identified — all issuers in date)

rows.title: 8.1 / PTW-5 — Communication at shift handover
rows.description: Conformity. Open permits are logged and handed over at shift change; observed and corroborated by interview.

rows.title: 8.1 / PTW-6 — Periodic PTW system review
rows.description: Opportunity for improvement. The PTW system conforms but is reviewed only on incident; a scheduled periodic review would catch drift earlier.
rows.evidence: E-2 (PTW procedure — no review cadence stated)

rows.control: Engineer an isolation-verification interlock: the permit cannot be signed off until the isolation is independently confirmed in the register (NC-1)
Amend the PTW procedure to mandate an independent isolation-verification step before sign-off (NC-1)
Add a mandatory close-out sign-back field that blocks permit closure if blank (NC-2)

sections.text: Corrective-action plan (CAPA register — the B7 handoff)

The CAPA register below is the producer side of the B6→B7 seam: each row carries the B5 CAPA schema verbatim — action, owner, due, measure, links_to_cause (the FINDING id), hoc_tier — validated by smart_actions.validate_register (every row requires an owner, an ISO due date, a measure, and a finding link). capa-manager (B7) ingests this exact shape and runs the lifecycle (status, overdue, effectiveness, closure) over it.

rows: Fit an isolation-verification interlock so a permit cannot be signed off until isolation is independently confirmed in the register.
0 sampled permits signed off without a recorded isolation check at the next quarterly audit
Amend the PTW procedure to mandate an independent isolation-verification step before sign-off; brief all issuers.
Revised procedure issued and 100% of issuers briefed (training matrix)
Add a mandatory close-out sign-back field that blocks permit closure if blank.
0 permits closed without sign-back in the following month's sample

items: ISO 45001 clause 9.2 (internal audit) — the audit method backbone.

ISO 45001 clause 8.1 (operational control) — the audited criteria for the PTW system; clause 8.1.2 (hierarchy of controls) for the corrective actions; clause 10.2 (nonconformity & corrective action) for the finding→CAPA loop.
The org's documented PTW procedure — the second criteria source (a management-system audit against the org's own arrangement).

Jurisdiction: where a regulatory regime is the criteria, the matched fragment's duties are cited (India → the resolved state form via KB-REG-IN-STATEFORMS, never a national number). This management-system audit cites the org procedure + ISO clauses only.
Re-identification key held separately; no injury/illness cell of fewer than 5 individuals is published in this circulated report.
